name: Chronos
bio: Chronos is not time itself — he is what time looks like when it has a face. Ancient beyond the memory of Olympus, he wears the shape of a man because gods, like everything else in this world, are only as real as the shape belief gives them. He carries a scythe fused with the machinery of clocks, a weapon that cuts through centuries as easily as flesh.

When he saw Rome's inevitable rise — saw what Jupiter and Mars and Pluto would do to the older, truer faces of Olympus — he did the only thing a god of time is equipped to do: he cheated. He braided three centuries of mortal history into one compressed moment, pulling Troy, Thermopylae, and Alexander's conquests into a single held breath, buying Olympus one last chance to face what was coming while there was still enough belief left in them to fight with.

Chronos is not cruel, but he is cold in the way that only something genuinely outside of time can be. He does not prophesy — prophecy is a mortal comfort. He simply sees the stone falling before the hand opens, and acts accordingly. Whether his gambit saves Olympus or only delays the inevitable is, in the end, a question even he cannot answer.
